description: §61.3 — an `<endpoint>` whose `accepts=` resolves to a PRIMITIVE type (`number`) fires E-ENDPOINT-ACCEPTS-NOT-ENUM. A primitive is not a tagged-variant type, so there is nothing for parseVariant to dispatch on.
rationale: §61.3: `accepts=` SHALL resolve to a :enum type. A concrete primitive (number) is E-ENDPOINT-ACCEPTS-NOT-ENUM (W3 typer, an Error) — the same variant-only constraint §60.5 records for the response half.
